question.text: Under Section 15Y of the SEBI Act, what is the limitation on civil courts regarding matters within SAT's jurisdiction?

answer.text: No civil court shall entertain any suit or proceeding in respect of matters SAT is empowered to decide, and no injunction can be granted
Civil courts can entertain suits if the aggrieved party chooses not to approach SAT
Civil courts can grant interim injunctions pending SAT proceedings
Civil courts have concurrent jurisdiction with SAT

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 15Y specifies that no civil court shall have jurisdiction to entertain any suit or proceeding in respect of any matter which SAT is empowered to decide. Further, no injunction shall be granted by any court or authority in respect of any action taken under the SEBI Act. **The Trap:** There is no concurrent jurisdiction — SAT has exclusive jurisdiction for SEBI Act matters. Civil courts cannot grant injunctions against SEBI actions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This provision ensures regulatory finality — SEBI actions cannot be challenged through civil courts, only through SAT and then the Supreme Court.
